name: Poached Apples in Wine
recipeIngredient: Zest of 1 large lemon, finely grated
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, combine the wine, sugar, and lemon zest. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
2. Meanwhile, prepare the apples by removing any stems or cores, leaving them whole. Add the apples to the boiling liquid and reduce the heat to low.
3. Gently simmer the apples until they are tender but still firm, about 25-30 minutes. Carefully remove 4 apples with a slotted spoon and place in a serving dish. Leave the remaining apple mixture in the saucepan.
4. Increase the heat back to medium-high and continue cooking the poaching liquid until it becomes syrupy, approximately 15 minutes.
5. Pour about half of the hot syrup over the cooked apples and allow them to cool to room temperature. Gently sprinkle any remaining lemon zest over the cooled apples before serving.
This recipe is a low-fat (30% or less) and low-sodium option, making it suitable for healthier eating. It serves 2 people.
